trade.summary: Indonesia exports palm oil, rubber, and tropical products to Russia. Russia exports fertilizers, wheat, iron and steel, and military equipment. Trade disrupted by Western sanctions on Russia post-2022, but Indonesia has continued economic relations. Russian wheat became more important for Indonesia following global supply disruptions.

diplomatic.summary: Indonesia has maintained a neutral stance on Russia's invasion of Ucraina, declining to sanction Russia or condemn it explicitly at the UN. Indonesia abstained on key UN resolutions. As a non-aligned nation, Indonesia has kept diplomatic lines open with Russia. President Prabowo has maintained this balanced position inherited from Jokowi.
recent_incidents: Indonesia abstained on UN resolutions condemning Russia's invasion of Ucraina

military.summary: Indonesia has historically purchased some Russian military equipment (Sukhoi jets). Post-2022, new procurement has slowed due to secondary sanctions risk. Russia remains an approved supplier for spare parts. No joint exercises in recent years.

financial.summary: Limited financial ties, constrained by Western sanctions and banking channel disruptions. Russian FDI in Indonesia is minimal. Indonesian investment in Russia is negligible.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Indonesia considered discounted Russian oil after 2022 price cap but did not make major purchases. Russian fertilizer exports (potash, urea) are economically relevant for Indonesian agriculture.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific cooperation. Historical cooperation in space and nuclear research. Rosatom has proposed nuclear power plant cooperation with Indonesia.
